3. Determine which label from the provided options best matches the Query Image.

- Base your decision only on observable visual evidence in the examples and the Query Image.
- Use the examples to infer discriminative visual patterns for each class.
- Do not use external world knowledge, hidden assumptions, or speculative attributes.
- Use only labels from the provided final options list.
- Choose exactly one final label.
- Output only the requested XML tags and not any aditional ones.
- Do not output any text outside the XML tags.
- Follow the condition-specific XML blocks first, then output exactly one final <response> block.
- The final line of the entire answer must be <response>final_class</response>.
- Never omit the final <response> block, even when another explanation block is required.

Rules must refer only to visible features.
Then check which rule(s) are best satisfied by the Query Image.

- observable feature: feature value
- observable feature: feature value
- observable feature: feature value
</features>
<kb>

- IF [observable visual features = feature value] THEN [class2]
</kb>

<rule_check>

</rule_check>

<response>final_class</response>

raw_response_text:
<features>
- animal type: dog
- fur color: light brown/tan
- fur texture: shaggy/wavy
- object in mouth: green stick-like object
- eye color: one brown, one yellow/amber
</features>
<kb>
- IF [animal type = dog AND fur color = light brown/tan AND fur texture = shaggy/wavy] THEN [35]
- IF [animal type = cat AND fur color = grey AND eye color = yellow/green AND body shape = rounded] THEN [27]
- IF [animal type = cat AND fur color = grey AND body shape = rounded AND location = sink] THEN [9]
</kb>
<rule_check>
- The rule for class 35 fires because the Query Image shows a dog with light brown/tan, shaggy/wavy fur, which matches the defining features for this class.
</rule_check>
<response>35</response>

<features>
- animal type: cat
- fur color: solid grey/blue
- eye color: yellow/orange
- body shape: stocky/round
- head shape: round
- ear shape: short, rounded tips
</features>
<kb>
- IF [animal type = cat AND fur color = solid grey/blue AND eye color = yellow/orange AND body shape = stocky/round AND head shape = round AND ear shape = short, rounded tips] THEN [9]
- IF [animal type = cat AND fur color = solid grey/blue AND eye color = yellow/green AND body shape = slender/athletic AND head shape = wedge-shaped AND ear shape = large, pointed] THEN [27]
- IF [animal type = dog AND fur color = light brown/tan AND fur texture = shaggy/wavy AND face obscured by fur AND wearing a bandana] THEN [35]
</kb>
<rule_check>
- The rule for class 9 fires because the Query Image shows a cat with solid grey/blue fur, yellow/orange eyes, a stocky/round body, a round head, and short, rounded ear tips, which matches all the specified features for class 9.
</rule_check>
<response>9</response>

- animal type: cat
- fur color: solid grey
- eye color: green
- ear shape: pointed, relatively large
- body posture: lying down, front paws crossed
</features>

- IF [animal type = dog AND fur texture = shaggy AND fur color = light brown/tan] THEN [35]

- IF [animal type = cat AND fur color = solid grey AND eye color = green/yellow AND ears = pointed and relatively large] THEN [27]
</kb>

</rule_check>
<response>27</response>
